title: Which countries include mumps vaccines in their national vaccination programs?
subtitle: This shows which countries provide and recommend mumps vaccines through routine services. People may still be able to receive the vaccine if it's not in the routine schedule – it might be optional or available commercially.
caption:
Which countries include mumps vaccines in their national vaccination programs? 2024

This shows which countries provide and recommend mumps vaccines through routine services. People may still be able to receive the vaccine if it's not in the routine schedule – it might be optional or available commercially.

The map displays the status of mumps vaccine inclusion in national immunization schedules across the globe for the year 2024. Countries are shaded to indicate whether the vaccine is administered across the entire country, not routinely administered, or administered only in specific regions. The vast majority of nations in the Americas, Europe, and Oceania are colored to show that the mumps vaccine is provided across the entire country. This includes the United States, Canada, Brazil, Argentina, the United Kingdom, France, Germany, Russia, China, and Australia. In contrast, a large contiguous block of countries across the African continent is shaded to indicate that the vaccine is not routinely administered. This pattern covers nations such as Nigeria, Ethiopia, the Democratic Republic of the Congo, and South Africa. Similarly, much of South Asia and the Middle East, including India, Pakistan, and Saudi Arabia, falls into the category where the vaccine is not routinely administered. Greenland is marked with a hatched pattern indicating no data is available for that territory. The legend specifies that a third category exists for countries where the vaccine is administered in only regions of the country, though few distinct examples of this specific partial coverage are immediately prominent compared to the two dominant global trends. The data notes that improved mumps vaccines became available for the first time in 1967, providing a historical baseline for the current distribution of routine administration. The visual distribution shows a clear divide where high-income regions and large parts of the Americas and East Asia have integrated the vaccine into routine care, while large portions of Africa and South Asia have not.
